title: REGIONAL STANDARD FOR COOKED RICE WRAPPED IN PLANT LEAVES Asia CXS 355R-2023

sections.section: REGIONAL STANDARD FOR COOKED RICE WRAPPED IN PLANT LEAVES Asia CXS 355R-2023
sections.summary: Document title only: Regional Standard for Cooked Rice Wrapped in Plant Leaves (Asia) CXS 355R-2023.
Adoption note only: indicates the standard was adopted in 2023.
Specifies the standard applies to the products defined in Section 2 that are intended for direct consumption.

Describes the product 'cooked rice wrapped in plant leaves', its types (quick-frozen, commercial sterilized, refrigerated) and process requirements including leaf selection/cleaning (no chemical reagents to alter colour; strings must not impart toxins), cooking, quick-freezing (see CXC 8-1976) with completion defined by reaching −18 °C at the thermal centre, sterilization to achieve commercial sterility per CXC 23-1979 (no leakage or swelling), and refrigeration per CXC 46-1999.

Defines 'cooked rice wrapped in plant leaves' as rice with optional fillings (meat, poultry, eggs, fruits/vegetables, beans, nuts, derived products, etc.) completely wrapped in plant leaves (e.g., indocalamus, reed, banana, lotus) used as food contact materials for flavour not consumption, and notes the product may be bundled before steaming/boiling and packaging.

Classifies the product into three types: quick-frozen (steamed/cooked then quickly frozen), commercial sterilized (sterilized to meet commercial sterility), and refrigerated (steamed/cooked then stored at refrigeration temperature).
Specifies the quick-frozen product is steamed or cooked before being quickly frozen and stored at freezing temperatures.
Defines the commercial sterilized product as one sterilized to meet the requirements of commercial sterility.
Defines the refrigerated product as one steamed or cooked before being refrigerated and stored at refrigeration temperature.

Outlines process definitions including plant leaf preparation (selection, soaking, cleaning, draining; leaves shall not be soaked in chemical reagents and strings must not impart toxins), cooking under appropriate pressure/temperature/time, quick-freezing per CXC 8-1976 to rapidly pass the crystallization range and reach −18 °C at the thermal centre, sterilization per CXC 23-1979 to ensure stability and no leakage/swelling, and refrigeration per CXC 46-1999.

Requires plant leaves to be carefully selected, soaked, cleaned and drained before use, prohibits soaking leaves in chemical reagents to change colour, and requires any securing strings not to impart toxic substances to the food.
States the product may be cooked using steam or other appropriate methods under specified pressure, temperature and time before being quickly frozen or refrigerated.

Requires quick-freezing to follow the Code of Practice for Quick Frozen Foods (CXC 8-1976), passing the temperature range of maximum crystallization quickly and not being considered complete until the product temperature at the thermal centre has reached −18 °C or colder; repacking quick-frozen products under temperature-controlled conditions is permitted.

States commercial sterilized products shall be processed before or after sealing in accordance with the Code of Hygienic Practice for Low-Acid and Acidified Low-Acid Canned Foods (CXC 23-1979) to prevent spoilage and ensure stability at ambient temperature, and the product shall not leak or swell after sterilization.
Specifies refrigerated products are those subject to refrigeration in accordance with the Code of Hygienic Practice for Refrigerated Packaged Foods with Extended Shelf Life (CXC 46-1999).

Lists essential composition and quality factors including permitted basic and optional ingredients, a peroxide value limit ≤ 19.7 mEq/kg for products with edible fats/oils or nut/animal-derived ingredients, general quality attributes (shape, uniform size, proper wrapping, characteristic smell/taste), defects to be substantially absent (broken wrapper/leakage, foreign taste, visible foreign matter), and that lots are acceptable if defectives do not exceed the acceptance number for an AQL of 6.5.

Enumerates ingredients: basic grains (e.g., glutinous rice, rice, millet, oat, barley) and optional ingredients including fruits/vegetables, nuts/seeds, preserved fruits, edible fungi, meat, poultry, aquatic products, eggs, edible fats/oils, beans, derived products, sugar, salt, spices, seasonings and other appropriate ingredients.
Lists basic ingredients as glutinous rice, rice, millet, oat, barley, etc.

Lists optional ingredients including fruits and vegetables (including pulses/legumes), nuts and seeds, preserved fruits, edible fungi, meat, poultry, aquatic products, eggs, edible fats and oils, beans, derived products of these, sugar, edible salt, spices and culinary herbs, seasonings and other appropriate ingredients.
Requires the product to comply with maximum levels in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and with Codex maximum residue limits for pesticides.

Recommends preparation and handling in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969) and other relevant Codex codes, requires compliance with microbiological criteria established per CXG 21-1997, and specifies that quick-frozen, commercial sterilized and refrigerated products must comply with their respective Codes of Practice (CXC 8-1976, CXC 23-1979, CXC 46-1999).

Requires net weight to be declared in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) and states lot net weight requirements are met when the average net weight of examined containers is not less than the declared weight provided there is no unreasonable shortage in individual containers.
Requires the product's net weight to be indicated in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985).
Net weight requirements are met when the average net weight of all containers examined is at least the declared weight provided there is no unreasonable shortage in individual containers.

Products covered by this standard must be labelled in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), with further name and non-retail container labelling provisions set out in subsequent sections (including CXS 346-2021).

The product must be labelled as 'Cooked rice wrapped in plant leaves' (or with the corresponding name in Section 2.2), though other names may be used under national law and custom provided they do not mislead consumers.
Labelling of non-retail containers should follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
Packaging for cooked rice wrapped in plant leaves must comply with the applicable provisions of the cited Codex Codes of Practice (CXC 8-1976, CXC 23-1979, or CXC 46-1999).
Methods of analysis and sampling for checking compliance with this standard shall use the relevant procedures in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999).
Non-substantive notes listing cited Codex publications and a reference to the Codex website for Regional membership; this section is a bibliography/reference note.
